title: Checkpoints, Write-Ahead State, Crash Consistency, and Resume · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in versioned crash-consistent crawl checkpoint and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Define which policy, seed, frontier, attempt, digest, report, and output states must survive and which can be recomputed.
Write bounded versioned state to a temporary file, sync by declared durability, validate, rename atomically, and retain recovery copies.
Return claimed or fetching items to an explicit retry state using attempt and idempotency rules after a crash.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Checkpoint Boundary
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 9756 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove state boundaries, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, policy-version admission, and resume reconciliation; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define which policy, seed, frontier, attempt, digest, report, and output states must survive and which can be recomputed.

Inspect the Checkpoint Boundary trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Checkpoint Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define which policy, seed, frontier, attempt, digest, report, and output states must survive and which can be recomputed. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Checkpoint Boundary, keep Checkpoint Boundary connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Resume Reconciliat…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-resume-reconciliation-1igpke" that uses case 3972 to prove state boundaries, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, policy-version admission, and resume reconciliation. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
